steps.title: Identify the positive signal
steps.content: The passage signals strong approval: the reception has been "very good" and the book won the Asian/Pacific American Award for Literature.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Reviewers have ______ the book — the passage says reception has been "very good" and the book won an award, so the blank must mean praised or showed approval. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — blamed: held responsible for something negative — contradicts the very good reception and award win. ✗
  • Choice B — applauded: praised or celebrated — directly matches a "very good" reception and an award for literature. ✓
  • Choice C — misunderstood: failed to understand correctly — the passage gives no indication of confusion, only positive reception. ✗
  • Choice D — overlooked: ignored or failed to notice — the opposite of winning an award and receiving strong reviews. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
